title: Writing - Part 1: The Fashion Industry
description: Your class has been discussing the environmental and social impact of the global fashion industry.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the issues in your notes. You should explain which issue you consider most serious, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: The fashion industry is one of the biggest polluters in the world, producing vast amounts of textile waste and using toxic dyes.
Many garment workers in developing countries are paid extremely low wages and work in unsafe conditions.
Consumers are encouraged to buy large quantities of cheap clothing that quickly goes out of fashion and ends up in landfill.

structure.introduction: Introduce the fashion industry as both a major global business and a source of serious ethical concerns.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first issue and its impact on people or the planet.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second issue and argue which is the more pressing problem.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your view and suggest how the industry could improve.

content.modelAnswer:
The global fashion industry is worth trillions of dollars, but it comes at a high cost to both the environment and the people who work within it. Two of its most serious problems are environmental pollution and poor workers' rights.

Fashion is one of the world's most polluting industries, responsible for enormous quantities of water consumption, chemical waste and carbon emissions. The trend for cheap, disposable clothing means that garments are worn only a few times before being discarded. Synthetic fibres, which make up most fast fashion items, take hundreds of years to decompose in landfill, creating a lasting environmental legacy.

However, I believe the exploitation of garment workers is an even more urgent concern. Millions of people, predominantly women in developing countries, toil in unsafe factories for wages that do not cover basic living costs. This human suffering is a direct result of consumer demand for ever-cheaper clothing.

In conclusion, both issues demand action, but we must not allow environmental concerns to overshadow the immediate suffering of workers who deserve fair pay and safe conditions.
